{"key_event":"Mandate centralization amid confessionalized mobilization","reasoning":"French vetoes, constitutional suspension, census administration, the Régie, and repression modestly increased coercive and administrative capacity, though Depression-era fiscal weakness and contested legitimacy kept the state only moderate. Civil society also gained through urban press, unions, parties, nationalist campaigns, and youth movements, but economic distress, sectarian fragmentation, and weakened parliamentary checks limited the net increase.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":5.4,"state_change":0.6,"state_power":5.6}
